title: Sum of Two Integers
description: Read a single line containing two integers separated by whitespace. Print their sum.
hints: Split the line and convert both parts to integers before adding.
editorial: Parse two integers and output a + b.

codeSnippets.PYTHON:
import sys

line = sys.stdin.readline().strip()
# read two integers and print sum

referenceSolutions.PYTHON:
import sys

a, b = map(int, sys.stdin.readline().split())
print(a + b)

title: Square of a Number
description: Read one integer n from standard input. Print n squared (n * n).

codeSnippets.PYTHON:
import sys

n = int(sys.stdin.readline().strip())
# print n * n

referenceSolutions.PYTHON:
import sys

n = int(sys.stdin.readline().strip())
print(n * n)

title: Sum of Digits
description: Read one non-negative integer n from standard input. Print the sum of its decimal digits.
hints: Convert to string and iterate, or use modulo 10 in a loop.
editorial: Repeatedly take n % 10 and add to sum, then n //= 10 until n is 0.

codeSnippets.PYTHON:
import sys

n = int(sys.stdin.readline().strip())
# print sum of digits

referenceSolutions.PYTHON:
import sys

n = int(sys.stdin.readline().strip())
s = 0
while n > 0:
    s += n % 10
    n //= 10
print(s)

title: Count Vowels
description: Read one line of input (letters only). Count how many vowels appear: a, e, i, o, u in either lowercase or uppercase. Print the count as an integer.
constraints: Length of line is between 1 and 1000, English letters only
hints: Normalize each character with toLowerCase() or check both cases.
editorial: Scan each character and increment counter if it is one of the ten vowel forms.

JAVASCRIPT.explanation: Letters e, e, a are vowels.

JAVA.explanation: i, e, i, e are vowels.

codeSnippets.PYTHON:
import sys

s = sys.stdin.readline().strip()
# count vowels

referenceSolutions.PYTHON:
import sys

s = sys.stdin.readline().strip()
v = set('aeiouAEIOU')
print(sum(1 for c in s if c in v))
